author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
Pelagius Deceived the Council in Palestine, But Was Unable to Deceive the Church at Rome.
title: Pelagius Deceived the Council in Palestine, But Was Unable to Deceive the Church at Rome.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Chapter 9 [VIII.]—Pelagius Deceived the Council in Palestine, But Was Unable to Deceive the Church at Rome.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Wherefore Pelagius, too, if he will only reflect candidly on his own position and writings, has no reason for saying that he ought not to have been banned with such a sentence.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: For although he deceived the council in Palestine, seemingly clearing himself before it, he entirely failed in imposing on the church at Rome (where, as you well know, he is by no means a stranger), although he went so far as to make the attempt, if he might somehow succeed.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: But, as I have just said, he entirely failed.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: For the most blessed Pope Zosimus recollected what his predecessor, who had set him so worthy an example, had thought of these very proceedings.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Nor did he omit to observe what opinion was entertained about this man by the trusty Romans, whose faith deserved to be spoken of in the Lord, and whose consistent zeal in defence of catholic truth against this heresy he saw prevailing amongst them with warmth, and at the same time most perfect harmony.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: The man had lived among them for a long while, and his opinions could not escape their notice; moreover, they had so completely found out his disciple Cœlestius, as to be able at once to adduce the most trustworthy and irrefragable evidence on this subject.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Now what was the solemn judgment which the holy Pope Innocent formed respecting the proceedings in the Synod of Palestine, by which Pelagius boasts of having been acquitted, you may indeed read in the letter which he addressed to me.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: It is duly mentioned also in the answer which was forwarded by the African Synod to the venerable Pope Zosimus and which, along with the other instructions, we have despatched to your loving selves.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: But it seems to me, at the same time, that I ought not to omit producing the particulars in the present work.
